risk.warning.security: This page performs active system changes (firewall, SSH, updates). This note describes the action type, not a current error state.
risk.warning.controlCenter: Network, display and device settings are changed here. Only adjust if you understand the impact.
risk.warning.webserver: Installing and configuring web servers (Nginx/Apache) changes services and ports.
risk.warning.nas: NAS setup may adjust storage and services on the system.
risk.warning.devenv: The development environment installs extra software and may affect system paths.
risk.warning.devDashboard: Development cockpit: internal overview, no automatic write actions — still for developers/experts only.
risk.warning.raspberryPiConfig: Changes to Raspberry Pi configuration (config.txt, overclocking, boot options) can damage the system or make it unbootable. For experienced users only.
risk.warning.mailserver: A public mail server needs DNS, SPF, DKIM and a secure setup. Misconfiguration can cause open relays or blocklists. For experienced users only.

dashboard.tooltip.sensor.vcgencmd:
What: Raspberry Pi SoC temperature (CPU/GPU).
Where: Onboard (vcgencmd).
Normal: about 40–70°C under load, >80°C critical – check cooling.
dashboard.tooltip.sensor.cpuPackage:
What: CPU package temperature.
Where: Onboard (thermal_zone).
Normal: often <90°C under load depending on vendor.
dashboard.tooltip.sensor.nvme:
What: NVMe SSD temperature.
Where: M.2 slot or PCIe.
Normal: typically 30–70°C; follow vendor specs.
dashboard.tooltip.sensor.gpu:
What: GPU temperature.
Where: PCIe or onboard.
Normal: often <85°C under load (vendor specs).
dashboard.tooltip.sensor.apu:
What: APU/iGPU or ACPI thermal zone.
Where: Onboard.
Normal: depends on vendor.
dashboard.tooltip.sensor.thermalZone:
What: Temperature sensor ({{name}}).
Where: /sys/class/thermal/{{zone}}.
Normal: follow vendor guidance (e.g. CPU <90°C).
dashboard.tooltip.sensor.generic:
What: {{name}}.
Where: {{zone}}.
Normal: follow vendor guidance.
dashboard.tooltip.disk.body:
What: Storage drive (HDD/SSD).
Where: {{where}}.
Normal: usage varies; >90% can hurt performance.
dashboard.tooltip.fan:
What: Fan (RPM).
Where: case/CPU cooler (hwmon).
Normal: speed depends on vendor and load; 0 RPM = fan off or not detected.
dashboard.tooltip.display:
What: Display output.
Where: {{output}}.
Normal: connected and resolution ({{mode}}) OK.

dashboard.backendError.title: Could not reach server
dashboard.backendError.timeout: The server did not respond in time. Restart it or check the address.
dashboard.backendError.connection: Could not connect. The server may be down or the address is wrong.
dashboard.backendError.other: Overview, system updates and many settings only work when the server is reachable.
dashboard.backendError.solutionBody: Run ./start-backend.sh in the project folder. Or as a service: sudo systemctl start setuphelfer-backend. If the server runs on another machine: Settings → General → set server URL (e.g. http://<IP>:8000).
diagnosis.local.step1: Check network and whether the device is reachable.
diagnosis.local.step2: Compare the server URL in settings with your setup guide.
diagnosis.local.step3: Start the backend or tap “Check connection” again later.
The web server likely failed to bind because the required TCP port (often 80 or 443) is already in use by another service.
In the overview, check whether nginx/apache is already running or another service blocks the ports.
Resolve the conflict (stop or reconfigure the other service) or move to a free port—often an advanced step.
Don’t re-run “Apply configuration” until the conflict is clear; use logs or documentation if unsure.
diagnosis.codes.backup_restore.verify_fa: Verification did not pass. That neither proves restore will work nor that it cannot—only that this check failed.
Do not start a restore until the cause is clear.
Pick another archive or create a new backup and verify again.
Check path and file permissions; use logs or support if unsure.
diagnosis.codes.BACKUP-SOURCE-PERM-032.t: Data backup includes unreadable source paths
diagnosis.codes.BACKUP-SOURCE-PERM-032.u: Setuphelfer attempted to access data paths that are not readable in the service context.
diagnosis.codes.BACKUP-SOURCE-PERM-032.a: Reduce data scope to non-privileged sources (no full/container scope).
Use an appropriate backup type for system/container data.
diagnosis.codes.SYSTEMD-NNP-031.title: NoNewPrivileges blocks sudo
diagnosis.codes.SYSTEMD-NNP-031.user_sum: The service context blocks sudo. Non-privileged data backups should run without sudo.
diagnosis.codes.PERM-GROUP-008.title: Group context missing
diagnosis.codes.PERM-GROUP-008.user_summ: Target permissions are set, but the running process/session does not effectively have the `setuphelfer` group.
diagnosis.codes.OWNER-MODE-023.title: Mountpoint has wrong owner/mode
diagnosis.codes.OWNER-MODE-023.user_summ: The target path does not match the expected model (e.g. root:root 755 instead of root:setuphelfer 0770).
diagnosis.codes.BACKUP-MANIFEST-001.user: The archive has no usable MANIFEST.json — deep verify (checksums) is not reliable.
diagnosis.codes.BACKUP-MANIFEST-001.acti: Create a new backup and run verify with mode=deep again.
diagnosis.codes.BACKUP-ARCHIVE-002.title: Archive damaged or unreadable
diagnosis.codes.BACKUP-ARCHIVE-002.user_: gzip/tar could not read the archive or the check reports broken compression.
diagnosis.codes.BACKUP-ARCHIVE-002.actio: Try another medium or copy; recreate the backup if needed.
diagnosis.codes.BACKUP-HASH-003.title: Hash mismatch
diagnosis.codes.BACKUP-HASH-003.user_sum: Content in the archive does not match SHA-256 values stored in the manifest.
Create a new full backup; archive content may be tampered or corrupted.
diagnosis.codes.RESTORE-PATH-004.title: Unsafe restore path
diagnosis.codes.RESTORE-PATH-004.user_su: The archive contains entries (e.g. path traversal) that violate security rules.
diagnosis.codes.RESTORE-PATH-004.actions: Repack or verify the archive only from trusted sources.
diagnosis.codes.RESTORE-RUNTIME-006.user: Restore was requested without a valid target_dir or pointed to a blocked target.
diagnosis.codes.RESTORE-RUNTIME-006.acti: For mode=restore, provide a valid target under /mnt/setuphelfer/....
Do not use restore targets under /, /media, or /run/media.
backup.externalTarget.priorityCaption: Preferred: external NVMe → SSD → HDD → USB stick → SD card. Not: root/boot/system disk.
backup.externalTarget.noAutoFormat: Setuphelfer does not auto-format or partition media and does not delete your data.
diagnosis.codes.STORAGE-PROTECTION-005.u: Under /media or /run/media a target is only allowed when it resolves to a clear, allowlisted block device (external preferred) — not root, loop, tmpfs, or unsafe mappers.
diagnosis.codes.STORAGE-PROTECTION-005.a: See docs/backup/BACKUP_TARGET_POLICY_EN.md; verify mount and backing device.
diagnosis.codes.STORAGE-PROTECTION-006.u: The service cannot traverse or resolve a path under /media or /run/media. There is no silent fallback — fix permissions/mount or operator approval.
diagnosis.codes.STORAGE-PROTECTION-006.a: Make parent directories traversable for the service user, or use an approved external mount with correct permissions.
diagnosis.codes.system.backend_timeout.u: The connection to the SetupHelfer server hit a timeout. The device or network may be overloaded or unreachable.
diagnosis.codes.system.backend_connectio: No TCP connection to the SetupHelfer server. Often wrong URL, stopped service, or a firewall in between.
diagnosis.codes.system.backend_other.use: The connection failed. Check settings and whether the device is reachable.
diagnosis.codes.system.shared.actions.ch: Match the server URL in settings with your setup guide.
diagnosis.codes.system.shared.actions.re: Retry later; on a Pi check power and storage—or use logs/support.
diagnosis.codes.unknown.generic.title: No specific diagnosis
diagnosis.codes.unknown.generic.user_sum: There is no detailed classification for this message. Review technical details or contact support.
Expand the technical summary and review manually.
rescue.storage.duplicate_uuid.title: Duplicate filesystem UUID
rescue.storage.duplicate_uuid.user_summa: Two or more partitions share the same UUID. Mounting may be ambiguous; often cloned disks or corrupted metadata.
rescue.storage.partition_table_unreadabl: The partition table could not be read reliably with available tools (permissions, device, or tooling).
rescue.storage.lsblk_empty.title: No block devices reported
rescue.storage.lsblk_empty.user_summary: lsblk returned no devices. Check cabling/USB/SATA or retry with appropriate permissions.
rescue.storage.findmnt_json_invalid.user: findmnt output was not parseable; mount state for the device is unclear.
rescue.storage.mounted_read_write.title: Block device mounted read-write
rescue.storage.mounted_read_write.user_s: A block device is mounted read-write. For rescue analysis this is a risk flag; no automatic writes are performed.
rescue.storage.mounted_inspected.title: Mount inspected
rescue.storage.mounted_inspected.user_su: Device is mounted; options were inspected read-only.
rescue.storage.partition_table_ok.title: Partition table readable
rescue.storage.partition_table_ok.user_s: Partition table could be read successfully.
As a fallback, the partition table was read via fdisk.
rescue.smart.critical.title: SMART: critical
rescue.smart.critical.user_summary: SMART reports a critical condition. Plan disk replacement and backup data.
rescue.smart.warning.title: SMART: warning
rescue.smart.warning.user_summary: SMART reports warnings or an unclear state. Further checks recommended.
rescue.smart.ok.title: SMART: OK
rescue.smart.ok.user_summary: SMART health looks fine (when supported by the device).
rescue.smart.not_available.title: SMART unavailable
rescue.smart.not_available.user_summary: smartctl is missing or the device does not support SMART. No assessment possible.
rescue.smart.command_failed.title: SMART query failed
rescue.smart.command_failed.user_summary: smartctl could not read data (permissions, driver, or device).
rescue.smart.invalid_device.title: Invalid SMART device
rescue.smart.invalid_device.user_summary: The given path is not a valid block device for SMART.
rescue.fs.fsck_error.title: Filesystem check (fsck) error
rescue.fs.fsck_error.user_summary: fsck -n reported an error. See evidence/logs; no repair mode was run.
rescue.fs.fsck_issues.title: Filesystem check: issues reported
rescue.fs.fsck_issues.user_summary: fsck -n reported inconsistencies or suggested fixes (analysis only; nothing was changed).
rescue.fs.fsck_ok.title: Filesystem check clean
rescue.fs.fsck_ok.user_summary: fsck -n completed without critical findings (read-only pass).
rescue.fs.xfs_repair_error.title: XFS check error
rescue.fs.xfs_repair_error.user_summary: xfs_repair -n reported an error or could not be interpreted.
rescue.fs.xfs_repair_issues.title: XFS: possible issues
rescue.fs.xfs_repair_issues.user_summary: xfs_repair -n suggests repairs would be needed (not executed).
rescue.fs.xfs_repair_ok.title: XFS check clean
rescue.fs.skipped_mounted.title: Filesystem check skipped
rescue.fs.skipped_mounted.user_summary: Device is mounted; fsck/xfs_repair was not run (safety rule).
Phase 1 has no read-only fsck/xfs path for this filesystem type.
rescue.boot.kernel_missing.title: Kernel image not found
rescue.boot.kernel_missing.user_summary: No common kernel file (vmlinuz/Image) was found under /boot or /boot/firmware.
rescue.boot.initrd_missing.title: Initramfs not found
rescue.boot.initrd_missing.user_summary: No typical initrd/initramfs file found. Some setups embed initrd—may be benign.
rescue.boot.boot_dir_missing.title: /boot missing
rescue.boot.boot_dir_missing.user_summar: /boot does not exist—the boot layout is likely incomplete.
rescue.boot.fstab_missing.title: fstab missing
rescue.boot.fstab_unreadable.title: fstab unreadable
rescue.boot.fstab_unreadable.user_summar: /etc/fstab could not be read (permissions or filesystem).
rescue.boot.fstab_parse_error.title: fstab line implausible
rescue.boot.fstab_parse_error.user_summa: At least one non-comment fstab line has fewer than four fields.
rescue.boot.fstab_ok.title: fstab looks structurally OK
rescue.boot.fstab_ok.user_summary: fstab could be read; only coarse syntax checks—no mount tests.
rescue.boot.layout_ok.title: Boot directory looks plausible
rescue.boot.layout_ok.user_summary: Kernel and initramfs were found in the expected boot directory.
rescue.network.no_interfaces.title: No network interfaces
rescue.network.no_interfaces.user_summar: No interfaces were reported (unusual on a running system).
rescue.network.no_ipv4.title: No non-loopback IPv4
rescue.network.no_ipv4.user_summary: No IPv4 address besides localhost—offline or headless operation is possible.

dashboard.updates.aptHint: Setuphelfer does not run updates. Open a terminal manually — and not while a backup, restore, or package-manager operation is in progress. Do not start updates before BR-001.
dashboard.updates.safetyModal.title: Show update instructions
dashboard.updates.safetyModal.body: Setuphelfer never launches a terminal or apt. Run the steps below manually in a terminal — not during backup or restore.
dashboard.updates.safetyModal.highRisk: Security or critical updates are pending. Install soon — but not before BR-001.
dashboard.updates.safetyModal.normalRisk: Only necessary/optional updates are pending. Postpone until after BR-001.
dashboard.updates.safetyModal.noUpdates: There are currently no pending updates.
dashboard.updates.safetyModal.deferredTo: Update postponed. Recommendation: check backup first.
dashboard.updates.reminder.active: Update is scheduled for later (24h reminder active).

dashboard.sysinfo.title: System information
dashboard.chart.diskTipBody: If usage >90%, check performance; move large files to another partition/drive; use logrotate; clean temp files (e.g. /tmp) regularly.
dashboard.systemInfoCard.title: System information
dashboard.installStatus.title: Installation status
dashboard.installStatus.tooltip.security:
Set baseline security:
- Enable firewall (UFW) and review rules
- Enable Fail2Ban
- Harden SSH (e.g. disable password login)
- Automatic security updates
dashboard.installStatus.tooltip.users:
Add users:
- Admin vs standard
- Strong passwords
- Optional: SSH keys, groups/permissions
dashboard.installStatus.tooltip.dev:
Install dev tools:
- Python/Node
- C/C++ (gcc/g++)
- Java (JDK)
- Git, Docker (optional)
- Editor/IDE (VS Code Server/Cursor)
dashboard.installStatus.tooltip.web:
Set up hosting:
- Nginx or Apache
- PHP if needed
- Detect and map sites/apps
- Optional: CMS (WordPress/Nextcloud)
- Admin panels (Cockpit/Webmin)
dashboard.installStatus.tooltip.music: Mopidy, Volumio or Plex – playback when the service runs.
dashboard.cpuGpu.title: CPU & graphics
dashboard.sensors.title: Sensors & interfaces
dashboard.drivers.title: System drivers (kernel modules)
dashboard.modules.title: Modules overview
dashboard.modules.security.title: Security
dashboard.modules.security.tooltip:
Recommended first:
- Enable firewall (UFW) and maintain rules
- Fail2Ban for brute-force
- Harden SSH (ports/keys/password)
- Automatic security updates
dashboard.modules.users.title: Users
dashboard.modules.users.tooltip:
Multi-user setup:
- Create admin/standard users
- Strong passwords & groups
- Optional: SSH keys / sudo
- Clean up users
dashboard.modules.dev.title: Development
dashboard.modules.dev.tooltip:
For development on the Pi:
- Python & Node.js
- C/C++ (gcc/g++)
- Java (JDK)
- Git, Docker (optional)
- Editors/IDE (VS Code Server/Cursor)
- Version info and doc links
dashboard.modules.web.title: Web server
dashboard.modules.web.tooltip:
Web hosting on the Pi:
- Nginx/Apache + optional PHP
- Map sites/apps
- CMS (e.g. WordPress/Nextcloud)
- Admin panels (Cockpit/Webmin)
- Hardening & certificates
dashboard.modules.mail.title: Mail server
dashboard.modules.mail.tooltip:
Only if you really need mail on the Pi:
- Plan domain/DNS (MX/SPF/DKIM/DMARC)
- TLS/ports/firewall
- Spam/brute-force protection
- External relay
dashboard.modules.nas.title: NAS
dashboard.modules.nas.tooltip:
Pi as network storage:
- Samba (Windows/macOS)
- NFS (Linux)
- FTP/SFTP (external)
- Share permissions
- Optional: guest access & encryption
dashboard.modules.home.title: Home automation
dashboard.modules.home.tooltip:
Smart home on the Pi:
- Home Assistant / OpenHAB / Node-RED
- Ports & autostart
- Add-ons/integrations
- Remote access (securely!)
dashboard.modules.music.title: Music box
dashboard.modules.music.tooltip:
Pi as music/media station:
- Mopidy / Volumio / Plex
- AirPlay/Spotify (optional)
- Ports & autostart
- Web UI links
dashboard.modules.presets.title: Presets
dashboard.modules.presets.tooltip:
Quick setups via profile:
- NAS / web / home automation / music / learning PC
- Installs matching packages
- Sensible defaults
- Fewer mistakes
dashboard.modules.learning.title: Learning PC
dashboard.modules.learning.tooltip:
For school/projects:
- Scratch, Python/Jupyter
- GPIO/robotics libs
- Electronics tools
- Math/science tools + links
dashboard.modules.monitoring.title: Monitoring
dashboard.modules.monitoring.tooltip:
Monitoring:
- Node Exporter, Prometheus, Grafana
- Dashboards & health checks
- UI links
- Basis for alerts/logs
dashboard.modules.backup.title: Backup & restore
dashboard.modules.backup.tooltip:
Data safety:
- Create backups (full/incremental)
- List backups
- Restore (with warnings)
- Basis for reinstall without data loss
dashboard.quickstart.title: Quick start
dashboard.quickstart.body: Use the installation wizard to configure your system in a few steps.
dashboard.panda.introTitle: Your starting point for Linux and Raspberry Pi
dashboard.panda.introBody: The SetupHelfer panda guides you step by step: check, configure and understand your system.
dashboard.panda.companionStatusHint: The traffic light on the panda reflects overall status from backup, restore, security and updates (green = OK, yellow = review, red = act).
panda.role.helper: Visual helper for beginners – explains steps and highlights safe defaults.
panda.variant.backup.tip: Create a backup before making larger changes to your system.
panda.variant.security.tip: Start with firewall, SSH hardening and automatic security updates.
panda.variant.diagnostics.tip: If something goes wrong, the diagnostics view helps you spot typical issues.
backup.verify.basic.encryptedInfo: In basic mode, encrypted backups can only be checked for existence and size. For a deeper check, the backup must be decrypted.

backup.verify.deep.promptKey: Deep verification requires the encryption key. Please enter the password/key:
backup.verify.deep.title: Deep verification of encrypted backup
backup.verify.deep.body: Deep verification temporarily decrypts the backup and checks the archive contents. The decrypted file is removed again after verification.
backup.verify.deep.label: Password / key for the backup
backup.restore.onlyOne: Please select exactly one backup for analysis.
backup.restore.preview.confirm: Do you want to analyse backup {{file}} in test mode? The system will not be overwritten.
backup.restore.preview.sudoSubtitle: Elevated rights are required for the test restore. The backup is extracted into a preview directory without overwriting the system.
backup.restore.preview.toastSuccess: Test restore successful – the system was not overwritten.
backup.restore.incrementalNotSupported: Restore for incremental backups is currently not supported. Please use a full backup.

backup.preview.safeHint: Note: This test restore is non-destructive. Changes affect only the preview directory, not the running system.
backup.preview.systemLike.title: System-like paths in archive
backup.preview.systemLike.hint: These entries lie in typical system or user paths (e.g. /etc, /home). Carefully check whether a productive restore really makes sense.
backup.preview.blocked.title: Problematic archive entries detected
backup.preview.blocked.hint: The archive contains paths or file types that were blocked for safety reasons (e.g. path traversal, absolute paths, symlinks, device files). This backup should not be applied productively.

That's often normal—for example while the app is still starting or the service isn't running yet. You can still open the app with “Later” and read the help. Enter the password later under Settings once everything is connected.

backup.messages.full_expert_confirmation: Full root backup (expert mode): enable the confirmation checkbox or choose another profile.

backup.messages.backup_target_root_files: Backups on the root filesystem (/) are not allowed. Use an external volume (e.g. USB under /media or /mnt).
backup.messages.backup_target_non_block_: The target is not a local block device (e.g. tmpfs or network filesystem). Use a USB/disk with ext4, xfs, or ntfs.
backup.messages.backup_target_live_files: The target uses a live/read-only medium (e.g. squashfs). Backups there are not allowed.
backup.messages.backup_target_filesystem: The filesystem at the target is not permitted for backups (only ext4, xfs, or ntfs).
backup.messages.backup_target_not_writab: The backup target directory is not writable for the Setuphelfer service. Use root:setuphelfer and mode 0770 on the mount, and ensure the backend unit has SupplementaryGroups=setuphelfer (see documentation).
backup.messages.failed_manifest_missing: Backup failed: MANIFEST.json is missing or could not be embedded; the incomplete archive was removed.
backup.messages.archive_corrupt: The archive gzip layer is corrupt or truncated.
backup.messages.verify_integrity_failed: Deep verification found integrity errors (see details).
backup.messages.error: An error occurred during the backup operation.
backup.messages.no_space: Not enough free disk space on the target.
backup.messages.write_io_error: Backup failed: write I/O error on the target medium while creating the archive. Check USB/SATA power and cables, kernel logs (dmesg), and whether the filesystem remounted read-only. Do not verify or restore the partial file as a complete backup.
backup.messages.slow_but_active: Backup is running — large datasets or a slow target can extend runtime significantly. See status for throughput and progress.
backup.messages.compression_bottleneck: Compression (gzip/pigz) may be the bottleneck; full-root backups are CPU- and I/O-heavy.
backup.messages.package_blocks_backup: Package or update activity is blocking backup start. Retry later or pause timers temporarily (do not permanently disable).
backup.messages.full_backup_expert: Full root backup is an expert mode — slow and resource-intensive. For daily use prefer a smaller profile (e.g. user data).
backup.messages.recommended_profile_hint: The recommended profile excludes common cache paths and uses faster gzip-compatible compression (pigz) when installed.
backup.messages.create_evidence_package: Create a diagnostic evidence package — see “Backup Evidence Collector” documentation.
backup.messages.eta_unknown: ETA: not estimable (no reliable total size).
backup.messages.eta_estimated: ETA is estimated and may drift if source or target slows down.

documentation.sidebar.heading: Documentation
documentation.intro.lead: Use the chapters on the left for guides, screenshots, and step-by-step instructions for each area. The knowledge base also covers the FAQ, experience level (beginner/advanced), panda companions, and guided flows — see the Dashboard chapter and docs/user/GUIDED_UX_AND_COMPANION.md.
documentation.bodyLanguageNote: The detailed text in each chapter below is still in German. Many topics are available in English on the website (setuphelfer.de — documentation, tutorials, troubleshooting). Full in-app translation of this manual is in progress.
documentation.nav.dualdisplay: Dual display DSI0 + HDMI1 – two monitors
documentation.backupRestore.fulRecoveryP: In addition to the UI, backend modules cover worst-case scenarios (raw image, manifest with checksums and partition dump, verification, restore, recovery menu). Details and paths:
documentation.backupRestore.fulRecoveryL: Modules: backup_engine, backup_verify, restore_engine, backup_crypto (AES-256-GCM; keys must never be stored inside the archive).
Recovery CLI: recovery/main.py (e.g. USB/WebDAV)—use on real hardware only after careful checks.
documentation.faq.backupEngines.title: Worst-case backup / full recovery—what is it, and where is it documented?
documentation.faq.backupEngines.lead: Additional Python modules for raw images, manifests, verification, and restore—independent of the standard Backup page.
documentation.faq.backupEngines.fix1: Overview and file list: docs/developer/BACKUP_RECOVERY_ENGINES.md; “Backup & Restore” chapter in this documentation.
documentation.faq.backupEngines.fix2: Keep encryption keys safe outside the backup—they are not written into the archive.
documentation.faq.backupEngines.fix3: After restoring to disk/SD: verify boot on the target manually (automated tests only use temporary directories).
